mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) against FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 2 months, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side recovery, which triggers positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a hit rate below 45% and a negative information coefficient over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3 percent forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields a negative Rank IC over consecutive 2-year periods, or positive forward returns occur in less than 40% of months with payroll increases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts signal strengthening residential demand and construction sector activity, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The housing starts signal generates negative forward returns in > 55% of observations during late-cycle expansions, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized supply and labor market expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in high-frequency regression tests.
